description: Create a content slide on AI arms race business considerations using a full-width comparative table across five regions with row labels for policy focus area, technology leaning, and business implications.

header.headline: The US-China AI race is forcing governments and companies to choose between rival tech ecosystems, or hybridize them, to balance technology security and affordability

rows: US National Security Strategy has put renewed emphasis on its role in the LATAM region; Brazil and Chile are both pushing for increasing domestic capabilities
Europe is pushing for more technology sovereignty, but they remain very reliant on the US technology stack; France, Germany, Netherlands are pushing for EU sovereignty agendas
Strong focus to develop sovereign AI economies (e.g., Saudi Arabia); Saudi Arabia has developed partnerships with leading US AI companies
India is making an assertive push toward model, and chip sovereignty; “Made in India” chip initiative focused on developing a local ecosystem
Countries in the region are having to deal with a more transactional US policy approach and greater Chinese presence

Strong reliance on US-origin hardware, with countries like Brazil subsidising domestic semiconductor production to avoid this; Plan for continued reliance on US ecosystems until regional players emerge; China will also continue to push to make inroads in the region

Expect continued reliance on US technology. Attempts to drive sovereignty have relied on US firms e.g., AWS European Sovereign Cloud; Some pockets of European firms will emerge; Companies should plan to drive more sovereignty across their technology stack and ecosystem

Expect further close collaboration between the GCC and western AI technology companies; Businesses should align their strategies to regional AI sovereignty strategies e.g., UAE G42 or Saudi Arabia HUMAIN; Still expect to see growth in Chinese models, use cases, and worker training

India’s tech / services ecosystem is closely integrated with the US; Chinese LLM adoption should grow in the country, but there is still caution between India-China ties; Expect more domestic initiatives to improve India’s AI ecosystem

Most of the region is reliant on US hardware / infrastructure; The software layer will see more duality in Chinese and US ecosystems; Companies should develop technology strategies focused on competing US-China AI ecosystems
